Swift witness-table/@_specialize/resilience-domain generics — the strongest unrepresented model (separately-compiled generics with opt-in guaranteed specialization), gestured at by F009 (the only workflow-confirmed dispatch-adjacent card) and listed as Open in the dossier.

The constructive question that arbitrates A1's core inference vs A3's core mechanism: can a shared/erased generic body soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations, and at what precision loss vs per-instantiation code? Checkable against LLVM semantics without benchmarks; no one has done it.

AOT realization of the hybrid: how .NET NativeAOT / Mono full-AOT compute value-type instantiation closure and when they fall back to universal shared code — determines whether D005 transfers to an AOT LLVM target at all.
All load-bearing dispatch cards (D001, D002, D003, D005, D007) are provisional direct extractions, not adversarially verified (caveats.md standing rule).

The N001-N006, A001-A010, C001-C006, and J001-J005 card series named in the debate ground rules do not appear in any of the five assigned files; all three advocates and the critic flagged this — the debate effectively ran on the D/F subset only.

Core/periphery fact attenuation: how optimizer-visible facts are soundly weakened across FFI/dynamic-linking boundaries (D008 class) is undesigned; under the soundness-inviolable rule this is a precondition for any closed-world or no-interposition commitment.

Dispatch through first-class functions and closures is unaddressed by every position, despite D009 documenting Function-typed fields hiding call targets as an optimizer defeat inside fully closed programs.

Relative importance of dispatch strategy vs layout, numerics, and concurrency for overall performance is not established — the corpus is aliasing-heavy by selection (caveats.md), so the stakes of this topic are themselves unmeasured.

Cross-unit instantiation strategies (rustc share-generics, instantiation deduplication) needed to bound the mono-only code-size and compile-time risk (D004 shows unit/LTO boundaries already stress the model).
result.leadingCandidate: Advocate 1's skeleton — guaranteed static specialization as the core's only implicit path, with dynamic dispatch available solely through an explicit, restricted (dyn-compatible, D003-style) construct whose stated cost model does not rely on devirtualization — carried forward with A2's no-interposition/finality module semantics as a compatible amendment, and with the 'monomorphization-ONLY' foreclosure of D005-style bounded sharing explicitly held open pending measurement.
result.rationale: Three considerations drive the ranking. First, all three advocates converge on guaranteed static specialization wherever representation differs per instantiation (A2 borrows D001 outright; A3 specializes all value types), so the genuinely contested residue is narrow: whether reference-path instantiations may share bodies, and what build/visibility model surrounds the core. A1's skeleton is that consensus core, and it has the most direct card support in the corpus (D001, D002, D003) plus the best-attested qualitative asymmetry: source-level guarantees vs conditional, revertible recovery (D007 negative scope, D008, F007, misleading-comparisons note 3) — an asymmetry that matters specifically because the charter makes performance transparency the goal and lets ergonomics pay for it. Second, the rivals took more structural damage from the critic: A2's central 'guaranteed devirtualization' claim has no card behind it (LLVM WPD is uncovered) and the position is a visibility amendment rather than an answer to the topic; A3's mechanism rests on two undecided priors (uniform reference representation, AOT instantiation closure), its D007 backstop is mis-transferred from C++ vtables and circular where it works, and it never priced its shared path against the F003 alias-obligation question. A1's damage, by contrast, was to auxiliary inferences (the inverted F009 citation, the uncarded alias-discharge claim, predictability absolutism) and to a repairable composition hole — its core mechanism was not undermined. Third, per ground rule 4 this cannot be settled now: the decisive specialization-vs-code-growth trade has zero quantitative evidence, every load-bearing D-card is provisional, the strongest workflow-confirmed card (F009) mildly points at an unexamined Swift-style fourth model, and four whole card series named in the ground rules were absent from the assigned files. Hence design_candidate_later rather than a decision: A1's skeleton leads clearly on in-hand evidence and charter fit, but it hardens into a design only if the verification program below fails to overturn it — in particular, if the F003-discharge check vindicates the claim that sharing erodes alias facts, and if code-size/compile-time measurements do not show mono-only losing in size-bound regimes. If shared bodies prove able to discharge alias obligations cheaply and bloat proves severe, the leader should shift toward a bounded-sharing or Swift-style annotated-specialization hybrid.
followUps: Adversarially verify the load-bearing provisional cards D001, D002, D003, D005, D007 (per the caveats.md standing rule) before any further weight is placed on them.

Run the construction-level check the critic identified as decisive: determine against LLVM semantics whether a shared/erased generic body can soundly discharge F003 noalias/alias.scope obligations, and quantify the precision loss vs per-instantiation code; record the result as a new evidence card.

Build a dossier on LLVM whole-program devirtualization (!type metadata, -fwhole-program-vtables, virtual-function elimination) with version-dated sources — the missing mechanism behind both A2's guarantees and A1/A3's backstops.

Build a dossier on Swift generics ABI (witness tables, @_specialize, resilience domains) and formally evaluate the F009-implied fourth model: IR-level generics with guaranteed-specialization annotations, combining A1's guarantees on marked paths with bounded sharing elsewhere.

Define and execute a measurement program with pre-committed decision thresholds: monomorphization vs shared-body vs dictionary-passing on representative workloads, measuring runtime, code size, i-cache behavior, and compile time; include rustc share-generics/deduplication data to bound the mono-only risk.
Gather evidence on .NET NativeAOT and Mono full-AOT instantiation-closure strategies and universal-shared fallbacks, to establish whether the D005 hybrid transfers to an AOT LLVM target.

Design spike on escape-hatch composition: generic methods on dyn interfaces (excluded by D003), instantiation at dyn/erased types, and heterogeneous generic registries — either close A1's composition hole or document its cost as a known limitation.

Resolve the antecedent charter question: does performance transparency mean a predictable cost model or a guaranteed-optimization floor — specifically, whether the compiler may silently devirtualize an explicit dyn call it can prove unique.

Locate and surface the N001-N006, A001-A010, C001-C006, and J001-J005 card series referenced in the ground rules; re-weigh this debate if they contain dispatch-relevant evidence (plausibly C++, numerics, and JIT material).

Design the core/periphery fact-attenuation mechanism for FFI and dynamic-linking boundaries (D008 class) before adopting any no-interposition or closed-world module semantics — a soundness precondition, not an optimization detail.

Add evidence and a design position on dispatch through first-class functions and closures (D009's Function-typed-field defeat), which no current position covers despite affecting iterators, callbacks, and async in every candidate design.

result.question: How should the language express non-interference (aliasing, mutation exclusivity) — the strongest optimizer enabler in the seed corpus? (Note: the 'strongest enabler' framing is itself corpus-flagged as selection-biased per misleading-comparisons entry 1 and caveats.md; the defensible framing is 'well-attested in selected sources.')

positionAssessments.position: P1: Ownership/borrowing baked into the type system; exclusive &mut-style references as the safe default
positionAssessments.strengths: Post-attack, this position retains the corpus's only adversarially-verified exemplars of machine-checked non-interference at per-reference granularity (F001: safe Rust makes overlapping compute(&x, &mut x) impossible) and the only attested static route to data-race freedom (C004: Send/Sync + exclusivity), which matters under the non-negotiable soundness rule. It has the only concrete pipeline architecture (F009: exploit ownership in a high-level IR before conservative lowering). Most decisively, the critic's own convergence analysis strengthens it: P2's sound (checked) variant rests on ownership for access types per F006, and P3 concedes ownership fallback wherever disjointness verification is hard — ownership machinery is the common denominator of every sound variant surveyed. Its advocate's concessions were also the most honest and complete.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Four attacks landed. (1) 'Only compiler-verified mechanism' is falsified by F001/F006 — SPARK's boundary anti-aliasing is also machine-checked, so P1's uniqueness claim was spin. (2) The 'design attractor / independent convergence' argument has no card on SPARK/Rust lineage. (3) F004 was overclaimed: 'naturally proves fresh-return' contradicts F004's negative scope (lifetime/provenance/escape rules required first) and memory-object-model.md's open questions. (4) The 'double dividend' is non-additive: the interior-mutability route to safe shared-state concurrency is the same feature misleading-comparisons #2 names as puncturing shared-reference noalias facts (plausible, uncarded — needs its own card). Residual structural weaknesses: coverage-frontier size (unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw pointers/callbacks/FFI) is unmeasured; Rust's aliasing model is unfinalized (caveats.md); 'UB-grade freedom on safe code' ignored C005's undef-not-UB racing-load semantics; zero quantified payoff; and exclusivity-by-default-everywhere is a one-way door priced by no benchmark.

positionAssessments.position: P2: Opt-in/default function-boundary contracts (Fortran dummy-argument / checked-restrict direction)
positionAssessments.strengths: Correctly locates where language design adds value over intraprocedural analysis: opaque call boundaries (F002 names the analyses and the hazard). F005 is genuine evidence that a shipped performance language runs a default-no-alias-at-boundaries regime the compiler may assume, with aliasing as the marked case (TARGET). Signature contracts lower directly onto the attribute half of LLVM's vocabulary (F003 noalias attributes, F004 return noalias). The checked variant has a real exemplar (F006 SPARK anti-aliasing), and the layering precedent (SPARK atop Ada) gives the reversibility argument some grounding.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic's refutation of the load-bearing premise is verified: F003's positive scope attaches alias.scope/noalias metadata to individual loads, stores, calls, and fences, so 'the optimizer consumes facts at exactly one granularity: the signature' is false and the 'lossless one-to-one lowering' argument collapses to half of LLVM's vocabulary. The position then equivocates fatally: the headline Fortran-grade assumed-conformance form is inadmissible under the soundness ground rule (F001 negative scope: depends on programmer truthfulness; F003: violation is UB, silently, with no diagnostic), while the admissible checked form imports ownership machinery at every heap-pointer case (F006: SPARK checks boundary contracts only atop access-type ownership and banned side effects) — forfeiting the simplicity and reversibility selling points, which belong exclusively to the unsound form. 'Decades of production evidence' mischaracterizes F005 (specification of what compilers MAY assume, not empirical performance data). The trust-boundary symmetry move launders an asymmetry: every ordinary signature becomes a UB-bearing trust point vs localized greppable unsafe blocks. No story for higher-order functions, callbacks, or globals.

positionAssessments.position: P3: First-class verified alias-set/effect annotations over named regions
positionAssessments.strengths: Only position that natively addresses the finer-grained half of LLVM's vocabulary (F003's named scopes/domains and per-access sets) and can directly declare the strongest known contract, 'returns fresh region' (F004). Its verified-annotation discipline is exactly the emission rule memory-object-model.md mandates. It was also the only position to flag the loop/element-level granularity ceiling — which the critic correctly elevated to a debate-wide blind spot — and the only one to gesture at hybrid complementarity with ownership.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Under the corpus's standing rule (design proposals must cite exact evidence cards) it is not yet a citable design: zero exemplar cards exist for any first-class region/effect language (caveats.md lists Halide, ISPC, Chapel, Mojo, MLIR as uncovered; no DPJ/Cyclone/Koka cards). F006 is stretched from restriction-checking (banning hard cases) to disjointness-proof (discharging them) — SPARK's decidability is bought by the bans. The 'near-identity lossless lowering' claim is uncarded and internally undercut: classifying every access into a region and proving membership is exactly the lossy projection step it accuses rivals of. The 'generated/ugly-by-design' premise is not in the charter (ergonomics-may-be-sacrificed does not entail machine generation), and the verifier — the position's entire soundness mechanism — has no feasibility, cost, or discharge-rate evidence. The separate-compilation attack on P1 was a strawman (ownership facts are signature-carried types). It concedes collapse into an ownership sub-discipline at the pointer-rich frontier.

keyTradeoffs: Trust-boundary placement, not existence: all sound variants converge on checked discharge; what differs is where the unchecked residue lives — localized opt-in unsafe blocks (P1), every function signature in assumed mode (P2), or verifier holes at FFI/callback boundaries (P3) — and the corpus measures none of these surfaces.

Fact coverage vs expressiveness cliff: exclusive-by-default references maximize the program surface carrying static facts (F001/F002 payoff classes) but push pointer-rich structures (graphs, intrusive lists, callback-heavy code) past an unsafe frontier of unmeasured size; boundary/region contracts keep the base type system simpler but deliver facts only where annotated/checkable.

Lowering fidelity vs granularity: signature contracts map 1:1 onto LLVM's attribute half but cannot express instruction-level alias.scope metadata (F003); type-derived facts cover more surface but project lossily (misleading-comparisons #2, #5); region annotations claim the metadata half but interpose an unevidenced verifier.

Sequential vs concurrency dividends trade rather than add: the interior-mutability mechanism that enables safe shared-state concurrency (C004's ecosystem) punctures the shared-references-are-immutable noalias fact — the same design cannot maximize both without a carded resolution.

Irreversibility under unquantified benefit: pervasive exclusivity reshapes every API and datatype; default-no-alias-at-boundaries becomes semantic bedrock for every compiled program; both one-way doors are currently priced by zero benchmarks (F002 negative scope; caveats.md).

Boundary/object granularity vs loop/element granularity: all three positions state whole-object or per-signature facts, but F002's payoff list includes loop-centric transformations, and element/iteration-level disjointness — what vectorizers consume — is entirely uncovered (caveats.md: arrays, layout, Halide, ISPC, Futhark, Chapel all missing). The debate may be scoped at the wrong granularity for an optimizer-first language.
evidenceGaps: Zero quantified performance data for any non-interference mechanism (F002 negative scope; caveats.md: documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude). No noalias-on/off benchmark deltas exist for any design.

Frontier size unmeasured: what fraction of performance-critical code falls outside each discipline (unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw-pointer density in ownership languages; undischargeable contracts in SPARK-style checking). This is the decisive variable in the critic's sharpest tension and no card touches it.

memory-object-model.md prerequisites unanswered (object identity, provenance, interior-pointer stability across moves, uninitialized bytes, unsafe/callback/signal/finalizer/FFI invalidation) — these block sound noalias/lifetime emission for every position equally.

LLVM pipeline reliability unmeasured: F003's negative scope conditions soundness on metadata surviving lowering and being consumed; no card covers preservation/exploitation of scoped noalias metadata through -O2/-O3, and the widely believed Rust noalias-toggling history is uncarded background knowledge needing verification.

Card ranges N001-N006, A001-A010, D001-D010, and J001-J005 named in the debate brief were absent from all six readable files; only F001-F009 and C001-C006 were available. If those cards exist, the debate must be re-weighed.

A fourth mechanism family is unargued and uncovered: C006 (immutability-after-construction/publication safety) was cited by no advocate, and caveats.md lists all functional/value-semantics routes (Haskell/ML/Futhark) as unverified — 'make it immutable' is the cheapest non-interference fact per F002's writes-are-the-hazard framing and was never on the table.

No cards on region/effect-system languages (DPJ, Cyclone, Koka, MLIR effect interfaces) — P3's entire mechanism family — nor on annotation error rates for restrict-like contracts, nor on contract/effect inference feasibility for internal functions.

Checker as trusted computing base: no cards on borrow-checker/contract-verifier implementation defect history or compile-time cost, so the soundness ground rule cannot currently distinguish positions on implementation soundness (vs paper soundness).
No position has a carded story for higher-order functions/callbacks (misleading-comparisons #2 names callbacks as a fact-destroyer) or for global/ambient state mod-ref at boundaries.

The critic's interior-mutability-vs-noalias tension and the source-model-to-IR race-semantics mapping (C003 negative scope, C005 undef vs UB) are plausible and load-bearing but not directly carded — both need dedicated cards before the concurrency dividend can be counted.
result.leadingCandidate: Position 1 — checked ownership/exclusive-mutation in the type system as the core non-interference discharge mechanism, expected to land as the core of a hybrid (signature-level mod/ref summaries as the lowering surface per F003/F004, SPARK-style checked boundary contracts, and a C006-style immutability-after-construction tier), with the exclusivity-by-default-for-every-reference commitment explicitly NOT yet settled.
result.rationale: P1 leads for structural reasons that survive the critic's verified attacks, not because its advocate's case stood intact. What fell: P1's uniqueness claim (SPARK's boundary anti-aliasing is also machine-checked per F001/F006), the design-attractor argument (no lineage card), the F004 'naturally proves' claim (contradicted by F004's own negative scope), and the additive 'double dividend' framing (interior mutability trades the concurrency win against the noalias win). What stands: F001 and C004 remain the corpus's only adversarially-verified exemplars of statically discharged non-interference and data-race freedom — the only mechanisms meeting the non-negotiable soundness rule with evidence rather than promise. Decisively, the critic's convergence analysis cuts FOR ownership rather than against a verdict: P2's central granularity premise was refuted by its own key card (F003 metadata is instruction-level), its admissible checked form imports ownership machinery at every heap-pointer case (F006), and P3 concedes ownership fallback at its hard frontier while owning zero exemplar cards — so every sound road runs through ownership-like discharge, making it the common denominator, not a bet among equals. This is design_candidate_later rather than research_needed because the mechanism-family question has a clear leader on current evidence; it is design_candidate_later rather than a decision because (a) no payoff magnitude or frontier-size measurement exists anywhere in the corpus, (b) memory-object-model.md's prerequisites block sound emission for all positions, (c) an entire unargued mechanism family (C006 immutability/value semantics) and P3's region family have no coverage and could still reshape the design, and (d) the N/A/D/J card series named in the brief were never available. Per the corpus's standing rule, this names a leading candidate pending verification; it does not settle surface syntax, defaults, or the hybrid composition.
followUps: Locate or confirm nonexistence of card series N001-N006, A001-A010, D001-D010, J001-J005 referenced by the debate brief; if they exist in the corpus, re-run this debate with them on the table.

Produce the single most decision-relevant artifact: controlled noalias-on/off benchmark deltas (e.g., Rust mutable-noalias toggle, Flang dummy-argument aliasing assumptions toggle) on realistic workloads, converting F002's payoff-in-kind into payoff-in-magnitude.

Measure the frontier: survey unsafe/UnsafeCell/raw-pointer density in performance-critical Rust code and SPARK contract-discharge rates, to size the 'discharged subset' each discipline actually covers — the variable the critic's sharpest tension turns on.

Treat memory-object-model.md's open questions (object identity, provenance, interior-pointer stability across moves, uninitialized bytes, FFI/callback invalidation) as a blocking prerequisite work item before any noalias/lifetime emission design, per its own emission rule.

Create adversarially-verified cards for the two uncovered mechanism families: (a) immutability/freeze/value-semantics routes (C006 direction; Haskell/ML/Futhark per caveats.md) and (b) region/effect systems (DPJ, Koka, MLIR side-effect interfaces, Halide/ISPC) — then decide whether a fourth-position debate is warranted.

Card the interior-mutability vs shared-reference-noalias tension and the historical Rust/LLVM noalias-toggling record (currently unverified background knowledge), since both bear directly on P1's coverage and on LLVM-channel reliability.

Prototype the hybrid architecture implied by the evidence: exclusivity-checked core + signature-level mod/ref summaries + self-owned high-level IR exploiting ownership facts before LLVM (F009 route), and instrument scoped-noalias metadata survival through full -O2/-O3 pipelines (F003 negative scope).

Specify the escape-hatch race semantics (C002 UB-vs-bounded decision for unsafe/FFI code) and the exact source-model-to-LLVM mapping given C005's undef-racing-load semantics — required residue under every position.

Investigate element/iteration-level disjointness expression (arrays, loop-carried facts for vectorization) — the granularity all three positions miss and caveats.md flags as uncovered; gather Halide/ISPC/Futhark/polyhedral evidence before fixing the aliasing model's granularity.

result.question: What integer-overflow and floating-point semantics should the language adopt?

positionAssessments.position: P1: Fully explicit numeric modes (wrapping/saturating/checked/unchecked as distinct types/operators; scoped, lexically visible FP relaxation annotations; no implicit strengthening or weakening of contracts)
positionAssessments.strengths: Its surviving core is the best-supported architecture in the debate: the target IR's contract vocabulary is real and per-operation (N002: unflagged add wraps, nsw/nuw make overflow poison; N001: definedness lattice governs transform legality; N004/N005: declared numeric/index promises with poison teeth), the validated optimizer surface consumes facts at per-operation flag granularity (N006, medium confidence), and the truthful-lowering discipline it enforces is exactly what F003 and misleading-comparisons entry 5 require ('IR mechanisms are conditional carriers of source facts'). It is the only position robust to either resolution of the open soundness-interpretation question, because it defers the default binding. Its concessions were the most honest of the three.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic's attacks largely land. The 'only policy' uniqueness claim is unsupported: the implementation warning's own truth-maker list includes runtime checks (P3's route) and 'source semantics' (P2's route), and compiler-inferred flags are an uncarded fourth channel nobody priced. The bare-'+' refinement concedes that P1 does not answer the actual topic question — whichever mode the default abbreviation binds to inherits that mode's full critique. Unchecked-assume-no-overflow as an ordinary operator with no unsafe/proof gating is a standing soundness hazard under the debate's ground rules, not a deferred risk (F001/F003: contracts sound only if obligations true). Only two of four modes have card-backed lowerings (N002); checked and saturating lowering plus all mixed-mode conversion rules are invention. The FP-scoping claim exceeds N006, whose negative scope excludes strict-vs-fast FP, and a global fast-math switch lowers to the same per-instruction flags, so IR granularity cannot discriminate scoped from global source designs. Finally, its direction of inference (IR vocabulary therefore source vocabulary) is exactly what caveats.md's scaffolding rule warns against.

positionAssessments.position: P2: No-overflow contract default (mandatory debug trap; release lowers to nsw/nuw with violation as poison; explicit wrapping/checked/saturating opt-outs; freeze-on-violation hardened mode as soundness fallback; strict FP default with scoped fast-math opt-in)
positionAssessments.strengths: Strongest IR-mechanism support for its release semantics: N002 (3-0) verifies the nsw/poison contract exists and is precisely specified; N001 verifies poison is lattice-disciplined deferred UB with freeze as a specified containment operator; N004/N005 show violation-becomes-poison is LLVM's uniform pattern for value, range, and index facts, so the design composes into one verification story; N006 shows the flag-sensitive transform surface carries Alive2-style audit discipline. It engages the implementation warning seriously (source contract + debug enforcement as the truth-maker) and F009 supports carrying the no-overflow fact into IR rather than erasing it at the source boundary.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its ground-rule compliance is achieved by choosing its own reading of 'soundness may not be sacrificed' — precisely the question the corpus lists as undecided — so the position's admissibility, not just its ranking, is contingent on a ruling made elsewhere. The strict-compliant variant (freeze-on-violation) is unpriced and under-analyzed: per N001 freeze yields an arbitrary-but-fixed value, which flowing into index arithmetic (N005 territory) still yields memory unsafety unless independently bounds-checked, so 'freeze = soundness fallback' establishes IR definedness, not language-level safety. 'Wrapping permanently forfeits the fact channel' overclaims against the uncarded compiler-inference channel. The debug/release split ships exactly the IR contracts ordinary testing never executes and doubles the N006-style frontend verification burden. Both load-bearing legs — nsw benefit magnitude and debug-detection efficacy — are conceded unmeasured, so 'dominates' claims have zero comparative data behind them.

positionAssessments.position: P3: Trap-by-default in all build modes (Ada direction); strict IEEE FP default; every relaxation an explicit, lexically scoped opt-in with unchecked behind proof/unsafe boundary
positionAssessments.strengths: The only default with unconditionally defined behavior in every shipped build, satisfying the strictest reading of the soundness rule without interpretive maneuver; keeps 'tested program = shipped program' with one lowering regime and one verification surface; correctly assigns the burden of proof to relaxations per caveats.md's standing rule and N006's evidence that flag-sensitive folds are where LLVM itself applies maximal ceremony; honestly cites its own cost evidence (N003) and names its retreat paths (F006-style proof-discharged check elimination).
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its centerpiece optimizer-fuel pipeline is half unevidenced and half card-misuse: N004's positive scope restricts range metadata to integer loads/calls/invokes, so post-check arithmetic cannot feed the cited channel, and no card shows LLVM recovering nsw-grade facts from dominating checks or llvm.assume — confirmed by direct card inspection. The trap cost is unquantified and its mechanism is extrapolated from N003's immediate-UB division example to defined traps, which the card's scope does not cover. The 'only truth-maker available by default' exclusivity reading of the implementation warning is fiat ('source semantics' is listed first and is P2's route), and the warning lives in a scaffolding file caveats.md says must not decide source semantics. The deterministic-in-all-builds selling point erodes under its own conceded relaxed-trap-ordering retreat, which reintroduces behavior divergence across optimization levels. The strict-IEEE plank cites N006 against that card's explicit negative scope and conflates 'no fast-math flags' with genuine IEEE conformance (constrained intrinsics/strictfp have zero corpus coverage), so it may promise semantics the evidence cannot show implementable as specified. It also imports EH-representation coupling (F008) if traps are observable. Process note: its claim that A/D/C/J cards 'exist in other corpus files per directory listing' suggests consultation outside the permitted read set.

keyTradeoffs: Optimizer fuel vs defined behavior: the corpus's best-verified numeric fact (N002, 3-0) is that LLVM's strongest integer-optimization channel, nsw/nuw, is intrinsically a UB-class contract whose violation is poison. Obtaining it by default requires release-mode UB (P2), runtime checks whose speculation/code-motion cost is real in mechanism (N003) but unquantified in magnitude (P3), or forfeiting frontend-declared facts on default code (wrapping) — and no card prices any of the three costs.

Declared vs inferred facts: the whole debate assumes optimizer facts must originate as frontend-emitted flags; compiler inference of nsw/known-bits/ranges from types and dominating guards is uncarded in both directions and could materially reprice all three positions (shrinking P1's mode-lattice payoff, P2's UB-default payoff, and making or breaking P3's post-check story).

Single semantics vs build-mode split: one deterministic semantics (P3) pays runtime cost everywhere but keeps the tested artifact identical to the shipped artifact; a debug/release split (P2, and P3's relaxed-trap retreat) ships IR contracts that testing never executed and doubles the frontend contract surface requiring N006-style Alive2 discipline.

Audit granularity vs default-question evasion: per-operation explicit modes (P1) maximize per-transform auditability at the granularity LLVM's own validation operates (N006), but the language must still bind ordinary '+' to some mode, and that binding inherits the full cost profile of whichever default (P2-style or P3-style) is chosen.

FP strictness vs unknown cost: a strict default is the only FP choice that cannot be semantically wrong, only slow — but 'strict IEEE' vs 'LLVM default unflagged FP' is an uncarded distinction with different implementability (constrained intrinsics/strictfp), the performance delta of fast-math is unmeasured, and the three advocates' convergence on strict-plus-scoped-opt-in is convergence of unevidenced priors, not corroboration.

Soundness-interpretation dependence: under a strict reading (every executable has defined behavior), P2's primary variant is inadmissible and the contest is P3 vs P2-freeze vs wrapping, decided by benchmarks; under a contract-relative reading, P2's nsw default becomes the optimization-maximal admissible choice. The ranking of positions flips on a normative ruling no evidence card can settle.
evidenceGaps: Quantified performance data, the debate's universal deficit: measured nsw/nuw benefit vs wrapping on representative workloads (induction-variable widening folklore has no card); trap/checked-overflow overhead in scalar and vectorized code via overflow intrinsics; freeze-on-violation mode cost; debug-build check overhead where CI/fuzzing actually runs. caveats.md explicitly warns documentation evidence does not quantify magnitude.

Post-check fact recovery: whether LLVM regains nsw-grade optimization from explicit overflow checks via dominating-branch reasoning or llvm.assume. No card exists; N004 confirmed not to apply to arithmetic results. This is decisive for P3 and material for P1's checked mode.

Compiler-inferred flags as a fourth fact channel (inference of nsw/ranges from types and guards): uncarded in both directions; discriminates among all three positions and against the shared assumption that facts must be frontend-declared.

Floating point is a near-total void: no cards on fast-math per-flag semantics or flag dropping, constrained intrinsics/strictfp, FMA/contraction, reassociation, rounding modes, FP exceptions, NaN/signed-zero handling, or reproducibility; additionally the 'strict IEEE vs LLVM-default unflagged FP' distinction is uncarded and changes what 'strict by default' even promises. Every N-card's negative scope and the 'Still undecided' list name this.

Language-precedent cards absent: Rust's debug-panic/release-wrap field experience, Swift's trap-always costs, Zig's split operators, C# checked/unchecked, Ada/GNAT overflow-check overhead, SPARK overflow-proof discharge rates, and C/C++ signed-overflow exploit studies — the direct real-world tests of all three proposals.

Broken card universe: the ground rules authorize citing A001-A010, D001-D010, C001-C006, J001-J005, but none exist in the five permitted files (only F001-F009 and N001-N006 do, confirmed by direct read). Concurrency cards (trap ordering vs memory model) and array cards (interaction with N005 indexing contracts) could bear directly on this topic. The phase-2 numeric workflow's stalled subagents mean absence of counter-evidence is a coverage artifact.

Specification soundness of relaxed trap ordering (early-firing/coalescible traps) and its interaction with LLVM's side-effect and speculation model — required before P3's performance retreat is coherent.

Contract behavior across inlining, call boundaries, and LTO for nsw/nuw and fast-math flags, and whether scoped source annotations survive lowering soundly (the F003 'obligations survive lowering' question, unexamined for numerics).

Interaction of each overflow default with index arithmetic and bounds checking: whether bounds-checked indexing subsumes or duplicates overflow checks, and how freeze-fallback or trap semantics compose with N005's inbounds/poison contracts — raised by the critic, analyzed by no advocate.
result.leadingCandidate: P1's defensible core as a default-agnostic surface architecture: a small vocabulary of named, truthfully-lowered numeric modes (wrapping, checked, and unsafe/proof-gated assume-no-overflow; saturating deferred) in which the compiler never emits an IR contract stronger than the source promise, with bare '+' defined as an abbreviation whose binding — P2-style contract default vs P3-style trap default — is deliberately left open pending the soundness ruling and pricing data. Neither P2 nor P3 can be named leading as a default: P2's admissibility depends on an unresolved interpretive ruling, and P3's optimizer-fuel pipeline rests on a confirmed card misuse (N004) plus an unevidenced fact-recovery mechanism.
result.rationale: The debate is dual-gated, and the first gate is not an evidence gap but a prior decision belonging to another topic. The corpus's single best-verified numeric fact (N002) makes LLVM's strongest integer-optimization channel a UB-class contract, which collides with the ground rule 'soundness may not be sacrificed' under a reading the corpus itself explicitly lists as undecided ('whether source-level UB is acceptable or should be replaced with proof, debug checks, traps, or unsafe-only assumptions'). Every position handled that collision by maneuver: P2 redefined soundness as contract-relative, P3 outlawed contract-UB by fiat while its compensating fuel pipeline failed card verification (N004's scope confirmed to exclude arithmetic results), and P1 declined to bind the default at all. Until the project rules on what soundness means for contract-violating shipped programs, P2 and P3 are incommensurable — no benchmark can rank a design against one whose admissibility is undetermined — which is precisely the no_decision condition. The second gate is quantitative: no card prices the nsw benefit, trap overhead, freeze-fallback cost, or post-check fact recovery, and caveats.md forbids design decisions from this corpus ('No final language design decisions should be made from this plan'; all N-cards carry design_generalization_confidence: low). What does survive the critique intact, because it is compatible with either ruling and directly instantiates the verified contract structure of the target IR (N001, N002, N004, N005, N006, F003, misleading-comparisons entry 5), is the truthful-lowering explicit-mode architecture — so it is named leading candidate as architecture, not as an answer to the default question. The FP question is not decidable at any level: the corpus contains zero FP evidence, and three advocates converging on strict-default from priors must not be laundered into corroboration.
followUps: Obtain an explicit project-level ruling on the meaning of 'soundness may not be sacrificed' for contract-violating programs (every-executable-defined vs contract-relative-with-enforced-debug-checks). This is a normative prerequisite decision, not research; it determines whether P2's primary variant is admissible, whether P1's unchecked mode must be unsafe/proof-gated (it should be regardless), and whether P3's uniqueness claim holds. Route it to a dedicated debate topic (source-UB policy) before re-running this one.

Reconcile the card universe: locate or generate the A/D/C/J card sets referenced by the ground rules (they are absent from the five permitted files), re-run the stalled phase-2 numeric subagents, and specifically pull concurrency cards (trap ordering vs memory model) and array/indexing cards (interaction with N005) into the next round of this debate.

Run the pricing benchmarks no card provides: (a) nsw/nuw-flagged vs unflagged arithmetic on representative workloads including induction-variable/sign-extension-sensitive loops; (b) trap-on-overflow overhead via llvm.*.with.overflow lowering, scalar and vectorized; (c) freeze-on-violation hardened mode vs pure poison; (d) debug-build mandatory-check overhead on test/fuzzing throughput.

Prototype the two unevidenced mechanisms that discriminate the positions: measure how much nsw-grade optimization current LLVM recovers from explicit checks via dominating conditions and llvm.assume (P3's pipeline), and how much it infers without any frontend flags from types and guards (the missing fourth channel). If inference recovers most of the benefit, all three positions are overpriced.

Commission a dedicated FP evidence phase before any FP plank is adopted: primary-source cards on LangRef fast-math flags (per-flag semantics, flag dropping, call-boundary/inlining behavior), constrained intrinsics/strictfp, FMA contraction defaults, reassociation, rounding/exceptions, and reproducibility — explicitly distinguishing 'LLVM default unflagged FP' from 'strict IEEE conformance', a difference all three advocates elided.

Add language-precedent evidence cards: Rust's debug-panic/release-wrap policy and field experience, Swift's trap-always costs, Zig's +%/+| operators, C# checked/unchecked blocks, Ada/GNAT overflow-check overhead, SPARK overflow-proof discharge rates, and C/C++ signed-overflow exploit studies.

Draft the default-agnostic mode-lattice surface specification (the surviving P1 core) as a design candidate for a later round: named modes with a truthful-lowering table (wrapping -> unflagged; assume-no-overflow -> nsw/nuw behind unsafe/proof; checked -> overflow-intrinsic pattern, noting this lowering is currently uncarded), bare '+' binding explicitly deferred, and mixed-mode conversion rules validated against InstCombine canonicalization behavior (N006).

Analyze the safety composition questions the critic exposed: freeze-fallback values and trap semantics flowing into index arithmetic under N005's inbounds contracts, whether bounds-checked indexing subsumes overflow checking, and (if traps are observable/catchable) the EH-representation coupling flagged by F008.

If a trap-family default remains live after the soundness ruling, produce a sound specification pattern for relaxed trap ordering (early-firing/coalescible traps) and verify it against LLVM's speculation and side-effect model — without it, P3's performance retreat reintroduces the build-mode divergence it exists to eliminate.

result.question: What compilation/IR strategy best preserves source-level facts for the optimizer?

positionAssessments.position: MLIR-first custom high-level dialect, progressively lowered to LLVM dialect encodings
positionAssessments.strengths: Survives the attacks in its kernel: it correctly identifies that any strategy must ultimately emit the corpus's only confirmed encodings (F003 noalias/alias.scope, F004 return-noalias) and that F004's negative scope demands lifetime/provenance/escape rules be discharged before emission — a pre-LLVM verified layer is a natural place for that. It shares F009's confirmed architectural lesson (semantics-preserving IR above LLVM, language-level passes before LLVM IR generation). It is the only position with a cost-mitigation story (dialect reuse per A001-A004) and an extensibility story for the heterogeneous provisional fact vocabulary (A006-A010: schedules, uniformity, uniqueness, domains, independence intent). Its concessions were candid and pre-empted most of the critic's attacks, which speaks to the position's self-awareness even where its rhetoric overreached.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic's attacks land on everything distinctive. The claim that progressive lowering is 'the only route the corpus offers' to sound noalias emission is false — no route is demonstrated anywhere (fit-gap alias row: TBD), and F006 gestures at a source-rule route belonging to the rival. The verifier-vs-droppable-metadata dichotomy partially collapses on its own card: A001's negative scope makes distinct_objects an UB-bearing trusted assertion at the MLIR level too; runtime disjointness is guaranteed by the source type system in every architecture, so MLIR-first relocates rather than discharges the trusted step. The completeness claim 'the facts worth preserving are known' contradicts caveats.md (aliasing-heavy by selection) and misleading-comparison #1. Its distinctive content — MLIR over bespoke — rests entirely on single-source provisional A-cards plus a fit-gap table that is all TBD with 'missing primary sources' for effects and layout, the exact fact classes the dialect is specified to carry. Each added inter-dialect lowering boundary reinstates the preservation hazard it cites against direct emission, and its 'scalar soup'/'standing hazards' language cited self-authored untested scaffolding as corpus findings.

positionAssessments.position: Direct-to-LLVM with maximal metadata (noalias/alias.scope, range/nsw, fast-math), facts declared in source near-1:1 with LLVM contracts
positionAssessments.strengths: Its target vocabulary is the best-verified content in the corpus: F003 and F004 are workflow-confirmed at high source-accuracy, and the best-attested fact class (F001/F002 non-interference) genuinely has direct LLVM carriers consumed by the passes that deliver the confirmed payoffs (register promotion, load/store elimination, reordering). It has the lowest engineering cost and the fewest lowering boundaries, and its version-drift surface is confined to one interface. The 'make the LLVM contract be the declared semantics' inversion is a real optimizer-first design idea enabled by the ergonomics allowance (honestly flagged as uncarded). The critic also shows the survival hazard everyone wields against it is shared by all three architectures post-emission, so a portion of the attacks it absorbed were over-penalization.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its argumentative spine fails inside the corpus: 'every optimization the corpus verifies is executed by an LLVM pass' is refuted by F009 (SIL ARC passes staged before LLVM IR) and F007 (HotSpot), which removes the license for 'direct emission is the shortest sound path.' Its evidentiary-advantage argument launders selection bias into architecture — caveats.md says MLIR is uncovered by selection and forbids design decisions from that narrowness. Most seriously under the soundness-non-negotiable rule: F006 is cited backwards — SPARK's facts are true by construction only because a verification toolchain enforces ownership/anti-aliasing/effect rules, so the card evidences the need for checking machinery between declared semantics and UB-bearing emission; without it the position rests on programmer truthfulness, which F001's negative scope flags and the ground rules ban. F004 ('stronger than any source-language precedent') and F005 (silent on pipeline architecture; its source documents Flang, an MLIR-based compiler) are overreached. Loop/shape/schedule facts have no confirmed LLVM home beyond droppable hints, and the position has no story for fact boundaries (unsafe, interior mutability, FFI) where real-world noalias miscompiles historically arise.

positionAssessments.position: Custom language-specific verifier-checked IR (SIL direction), lowered once late into LLVM attributes/metadata
positionAssessments.strengths: The most direct instantiation of the corpus's only confirmed IR-strategy card: F009 is a production existence proof that an SSA-form, language-specific IR keeps source semantics (ownership, explicit ARC ops) visible long enough to optimize, with language-level passes seeing what LLVM would not. Structural carriage of the confirmed fact classes (F001 non-interference, F006 ownership/effect purity) makes preservation the default inside the language-level pipeline and gives F004's lifetime/provenance/escape proof obligations a natural discharge point before UB-bearing contracts are emitted. The position priced its own cost honestly ('maximal engineering cost, earned label') and its concessions anticipated the strongest counterattacks.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its load-bearing architectural refinement is uncarded: no card describes the SIL-to-LLVM boundary, its auditability, or single-boundary superiority — and the critic correctly notes the rival is also a single emission point, so the true differentiator (a verified IR preceding emission) is narrower than the rhetoric. Survival symmetry is unanswered: post-boundary, its emitted metadata is exactly as strippable as anyone's; the only escape (doing enough optimization above LLVM that survival matters less) is never made explicit, and whether this language even needs SIL-style language-specific passes is undetermined. F008 is generalized against its own low generalization-confidence label and its funclet example is ABI-dictated, demonstrating inherited backend constraints rather than deferral freedom. 'Fits by construction' is a slogan, and the corpus's one custom-IR maintenance data point — F009's negative scope: stale SIL docs and OSSA phase confusion at Apple-scale resources — cuts against small-team feasibility. Cost, compile-time, and the diagnostics benefit are entirely unevidenced, and the position concedes an MLIR-hosted dialect may dominate it, blurring its distinctive content.

keyTradeoffs: Emission soundness vs survival (the critic's cleanest cut): the three architectures differ only on where emission soundness is established — source rules, dialect verifier, or custom-IR verifier — and are identical on post-emission survival through the same LLVM mid-end to the same consumers. The hazard most rhetorically emphasized in the debate discriminates least between positions; the hazard that discriminates (who checks facts before UB-bearing emission) is where the corpus is thinnest.

Verified coverage vs architectural fit: Direct-to-LLVM has the best-verified target encodings (F003/F004) but the weakest checked-soundness story (F006 read correctly evidences a checking layer it lacks); the pre-LLVM-IR positions have the only confirmed architectural precedent (F009) but zero verified support for their distinctive infrastructure claims (MLIR reuse: provisional A-cards + all-TBD fit-gap; bespoke: N=1 with adverse maintenance caveat).

Engineering cost vs fact expressiveness: a pre-LLVM IR buys structural carriage of facts with no LLVM home (iteration spaces, shape, schedules — A002/A003/A006, all provisional) and a venue for language-specific passes, at unmeasured but real maintenance cost (F009 negative scope); direct emission is cheap and drift-minimal but caps the fact vocabulary at LLVM attributes/metadata and droppable loop hints.

Division of optimization labor is the hidden prior decision: if major restructuring (tiling, fusion, vectorization legality) happens above LLVM, metadata survival matters less; if delegated to LoopVectorize and friends, survival is everything. The three positions implicitly assume different splits; this design choice partly determines the IR answer and precedes it.

Reuse vs boundary multiplication: MLIR infrastructure reuse lowers build cost but multiplies lowering boundaries (each a fact-loss and miscompile surface) and inherits upstream dialect drift; a bespoke IR minimizes drift coupling but forfeits shared verifiers/passes and must sustain its own spec — and soundness rot in an under-maintained custom IR is a miscompile vector, not an ergonomics problem.

Trusted-assertion location is invariant: in all three architectures the source type system is the actual guarantor of runtime disjointness — verifiers enforce derivation discipline, not memory truths. This compresses the real stakes of the choice relative to the advocates' rhetoric: the decision is about where checking and optimization live, not about who escapes the F003 obligations.
evidenceGaps: All MLIR cards (A001-A005, plus A006-A010 for the extended fact vocabulary) are single-source direct extractions, never adversarially verified or version-pinned; the MLIR-first position's distinctive content rests entirely on them.

The fit-gap matrix (implementation/mlir-dialect-fit-gap.md) is entirely TBD, with 'missing primary sources' for effects and layout — whether ownership/effects facts have any sound MLIR home is unmapped, not merely unverified.

The pass-consumer matrix is untested self-authored scaffolding: every version cell reads 'LLVM version TBD' and no validation test (FileCheck metadata survival, negative alias, allocation elision, vectorization remarks) has been run; the debate's most-repeated 'facts' (lowering strips metadata, false noalias miscompiles) are hypothesis cells, though F003's confirmed negative scope independently establishes the conditionality in principle.

Zero coverage of the most decision-relevant precedents: Mojo and Flang/FIR (production MLIR-first compilers), Rust MIR, and Rust's history of toggling noalias emission due to LLVM miscompiles — the last bears directly on direct-emission soundness risk.

No quantified evidence anywhere on performance magnitude, compile-time cost, code size, or engineering/maintenance cost of any strategy (caveats.md); 'maximal engineering cost' and 'reuse payoff' are both asserted, not measured.
Direct-to-LLVM's encoding set beyond noalias — TBAA, range, nsw, fast-math flags — has no dedicated cards; only noalias/alias.scope is confirmed.

No card addresses fact boundaries and escape hatches (unsafe regions, interior mutability, FFI-poisoned memory): how each strategy represents where facts do NOT hold and suppresses emission there — historically the locus of real noalias miscompiles.
Whether this language will have SIL-analogue language-specific semantics requiring their own optimization passes is undetermined; F009's weight depends heavily on the answer.
The diagnostics/performance-transparency advantage claimed for a language-level IR has no supporting card.

Charter/inventory mismatch: the debate charter references card series N001-N006, D001-D010, C001-C006, and J001-J005, none of which exist in the seven readable corpus files; this ruling weighed only F001-F009 and A001-A010, and the decision record must state that the absent series were never weighed.
result.leadingCandidate: A verifier-checked, semantics-preserving IR above LLVM that carries ownership/non-interference/effect (and provisionally shape/loop) facts structurally and derives LLVM noalias/alias.scope/attribute encodings late — the convergent kernel of the Custom-IR (SIL direction) and MLIR-first positions, with F009 anchoring the kernel and the MLIR-hosted dialect as the unverified cost-reduction variant; the hosting choice (bespoke vs MLIR) is expressly not decided.
result.rationale: The confirmed corpus supports a direction, not an architecture. Its two confirmed anchors are complementary rather than conflicting — F009 (the only confirmed IR-strategy card) says keep source-level facts in an SSA IR above LLVM long enough to optimize them; F003/F004 (the only confirmed encoding cards) say the payoff contracts live below as UB-bearing promises that are sound only if frontend obligations are true and metadata survives lowering. Jointly they sketch the kernel shared by positions 1 and 3: verified facts above, derived contracts below. Pure direct-to-LLVM is weakened at card level, not just rhetorically: its universal premise is refuted by F009/F007, its evidentiary advantage is a selection artifact caveats.md explicitly forbids using, and its own F006 citation, read correctly, evidences the checking layer it omits — under the non-negotiable soundness rule, uncheckable programmer-trusted UB contracts (F001 negative scope) are disqualifying without added machinery, at which point the position converges toward its rivals. But naming the kernel the leader is as far as this corpus goes. Every discriminating question — MLIR vs bespoke, metadata survival rates, whether ownership/effects fit any MLIR dialect, engineering cost, the division of optimization labor — sits in cells marked TBD, provisional, or 'missing primary sources'; the pass-consumer matrix everyone cited is untested self-authored scaffolding; and four card series named in the charter do not exist in the readable corpus. The critic is right that the corpus currently yields a fact vocabulary, a soundness-obligation ledger, and an experiment list, not an architecture selection. caveats.md's standing rule ('no final language design decisions from this plan') and ground rule 4 make research_needed the only honest outcome: the evidence is too thin to select even a prototype target confidently, and much of what is missing (A-card verification, Mojo/Flang/Rust-noalias carding, fit-gap primary sources) is cheap documentary research that should precede spending on prototypes. I chose research_needed over prototype_needed because the fit-gap table lacks primary sources entirely, over evidence_conflict because F009 and F003/F004 compose rather than collide, over design_candidate_later because the leading kernel's distinctive claims are all unverified and it merges two positions rather than crowning one, and over no_decision because the positions are commensurable — they share consumers, obligations, and most soundness structure — once the division-of-labor prior is surfaced.
followUps: Adversarially verify A001-A005 (and A006-A010) with the same 3-0 treatment as the F-cards, pinning MLIR/LLVM versions; upgrade to confirmed or demote explicitly.

Fill the fit-gap matrix with primary sources: for alias/noalias, layout/shape, effects — and add an ownership row — establish where each fact lives in MLIR, which upstream passes preserve vs drop it, and what verifier obligations attach.

Card the missing precedents: Mojo and Flang/FIR (production MLIR-first fact preservation, maintenance cost, compile time), Rust MIR (custom mid-level IR without MLIR), and Rust's noalias enable/disable/miscompile history (direct evidence on frontend-emitted-attribute soundness risk).

Run the pass-consumer matrix's own validation tests on a pinned LLVM version: FileCheck metadata-survival tests, negative-alias tests, allocation-elision tests, vectorization-remark tests; record per-pass strip/preserve behavior to convert hypothesis cells into evidence.

Build a minimal two-pipeline spike: identical source facts emitted (a) directly as LLVM attributes/metadata from a thin frontend and (b) through a small verified dialect/IR lowering; measure fact-survival rates and optimization outcomes on one aliasing-heavy and one loop-restructuring workload — the comparative baseline every advocate's gap list demands.

Draft the architecture-invariant artifacts now, since they are needed under any outcome: the candidate fact vocabulary, the soundness-obligation ledger (per-fact truth conditions per F003/F004 negative scopes), and the fact-boundary/escape-hatch representation (unsafe regions, interior mutability, FFI) specifying where emission must be suppressed.

Surface and decide (or explicitly defer with a date) the division-of-optimization-labor question: which transformations the language-level optimizer owns versus delegates to LLVM — this prior partly determines the IR strategy and was the debate's hidden variable.

Determine whether the language will have SIL-analogue language-specific semantics (ownership moves, effect-driven rewrites, array restructuring) requiring dedicated passes; this fixes how much weight F009 carries in the final decision.
Card TBAA, range, nsw, and fast-math flag soundness conditions and consumers to close the 'maximal metadata' evidence gap.
Locate or formally retire the N/D/C/J card series referenced in the debate charter, and record in the decision log that this debate weighed only F001-F009 and A001-A010.

result.question: What race semantics and sharing model should the language adopt?

positionAssessments.position: Position 1: Type-enforced data-race freedom (Send/Sync-like capabilities + exclusive borrowing; races unrepresentable in safe code)
positionAssessments.strengths: The only position whose central mechanism is continuous with the corpus's strongest evidence tier: workflow-confirmed F001 (language-level non-interference rules as optimizer enablers), F002 (value = proving non-interference around writes), and F003 (LLVM noalias encodings exist but are UB-if-false, which argues for mechanically checked rather than asserted facts). C004 provides a shipped existence proof (Rust). It is the only design that plausibly gets UB-column optimizer freedom (per C002) with compiler-checked rather than programmer-promised DRF, satisfying the charter's soundness-is-inviolable rule for the safe fragment. It survived the critic best: the attacks that landed (C003 register-caching broadened beyond its volatile/atomic scope, 'theorem'/'provably'/'only' tier inflation over provisional Nomicon-sourced C004, F007 cited against a strawman, C006 stretched) target rhetorical overreach, not the mechanism itself, and the position's own concessions had already flagged most of them.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: Its load-bearing soundness claim rests on the weakest evidence in its deck: C004 is a provisional direct-extraction card from an explanatory source (caveats.md: the Nomicon is 'explanatory rather than a complete formal aliasing model'); no formal safe-fragment DRF theorem (RustBelt/Stacked-Tree-Borrows-class) is carded. The critic correctly showed its binary partition (provably-unshared vs explicitly-shared) omits at least two states its own cards imply: Sync-shared immutable memory (invented writes still illegal per C003) and interior-mutability inside safe types — exactly where caveats.md says the formal model is unsettled. Because emitted noalias/NotAtomic facts are UB-if-false (F003, C005), any type-system hole is a silent miscompile engine, and the position's UB-vs-bounded question is relocated into the unsafe fragment, not resolved. Its implied cost model was inflated: C003's restrictions bind memory models generally, so the true UB-vs-bounded delta on ordinary memory is narrower than the case priced, and unquantified either way (C002 negative scope). Heavy, near-irreversible design coupling to ownership across the whole language.

positionAssessments.position: Position 2: DRF-SC with bounded racy outcomes (Go/Java direction; sharing proofs as optimization gradient, detect-and-halt legal)
positionAssessments.strengths: The only position that takes soundness-under-bugs seriously for code where non-sharing cannot be proven: C002 documents bounded races as a shipped, deliberate design (Java 'for security', Go with implementation constraints and race-detection halts) that caps blast radius. It correctly identified that the rival positions' headline performance advantage is an evidence gap (C002 negative scope: no quantification in either direction), and it was the only advocate to quote C003's register-caching scope faithfully and the only one to flag residue-specifiability risk (out-of-thin-air, mixed-size coherence) at all — a problem the critic rightly notes afflicts all three escape hatches. Its fast-path recovery mechanism (type-proven non-sharing per C004 lowered via F003) is the same convergent mechanism the other positions use.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic exposed a structural incoherence the case never resolves: under F003, wrong non-sharing annotations are miscompiles, not slowdowns, so the fast path requires Rust-strength checking — at which point 'simpler types' is forfeited and the position collapses into Position 1 plus a residue tax — or the fast path cannot be emitted and the performance story evaporates. Its soundness headline is also undelivered as stated: 'arbitrary-but-memory-safe wrong answers' is uncarded, and bounded outcomes preserve memory safety only if safety-critical multiword values (fat pointers, slices, vtable words) cannot tear — an unpriced layout/atomicity constraint no card covers. Leg (c) of its argument (LLVM's model 'sits closer to bounded') is directly contradicted by C003's negative scope (undef racing loads are not a bounded source semantics), so faithful lowering likely needs stronger-than-NotAtomic orderings whose cost C005 shows is real and nothing quantifies. Detect-and-halt is permission, not obligation, with unmeasured runtime cost.

positionAssessments.position: Position 3: Message-passing core (no shared mutable memory in core; move-on-send ownership transfer; atomics confined to an explicit systems module)
positionAssessments.strengths: Proposes the strongest structural invariant available for the F001/F002 non-interference theme: single ownership at every program point makes core memory unshared by construction, discharging F003's frontend obligations without programmer promises. F006 (SPARK ownership-transfer, workflow-confirmed) and C004 (Rust Send) make checked move-on-send feasible rather than speculative. Confining the C002 UB-vs-bounded question to a small, explicitly-marked module is a genuine surface-area reduction even if not a resolution. The case was unusually honest in self-flagging its extrapolations (F004 receive-disjointness analogy) and its uncarded exemplars.
positionAssessments.weaknesses: The critic dismantled its differentiation from Position 1: the C006 immutable-publication carve-out reintroduces shared memory into the core (falsifying 'every core-language access lowers as NotAtomic' and 'DRF-SC holds trivially'), and policing that carve-out plus residual-alias-free moves (raw pointers, FFI survivors) is borrow-checker-shaped machinery the rhetoric claimed to avoid. Canonical optimizer-first workloads — fork-join mutation of disjoint slices of one array — are inexpressible without deep copies or split-ownership machinery, and shared-state algorithms migrate wholesale to the systems module, where all C003 costs plus the unresolved semantics reappear (leakage risk making the design worst-of-both-worlds). Its closest real exemplars (Erlang, Pony, Swift actors) have zero cards — explicitly Open in the implementation file — so the position argues entirely from adjacent evidence. Dismissing throughput/expressiveness objections as 'ergonomic' misused the charter: those are performance objections squarely in scope. The shared-memory runtime kernel (channels, scheduler) underneath every core guarantee is unspecified.

keyTradeoffs: Residue semantics is the real decision: all three positions converge on the identical fast path (statically checked non-sharing facts lowered to F003 noalias / C005 NotAtomic, both UB-if-false), so the debate reduces to what a race means in the unproven residue — P1's unsafe fragment, P2's possibly-shared remainder, P3's systems module. UB residue keeps maximal optimizer freedom but makes every guarantee conditional on race-freedom the design cannot check there; bounded residue caps blast radius but pays C003-class restrictions of unquantified magnitude (C002 negative scope). Each position relocates C002's dilemma rather than resolving it.

Checker strength vs fast-path soundness: because emitted non-sharing facts are UB-if-false (F003), any design wanting the fast path needs mechanically sound proofs (type system, SPARK-style verification per F006, or structural ownership). There is no 'simpler types + full optimization' point in the space — Position 2's attempt to occupy it was shown incoherent. Weaker checking means either forgoing noalias emission (losing the F002 optimization classes) or risking silent miscompiles.

Structural restriction vs expressiveness: single-owner message passing maximizes provable non-interference but taxes shared-state and data-parallel workloads (disjoint-slice mutation, concurrent maps, work-stealing), pushing exactly the performance-critical code into the escape hatch where the guarantee is void — so stronger core invariants can yield weaker whole-program coverage.

Bounded-outcome soundness vs layout freedom: bounded races preserve memory safety only if safety-critical values cannot tear, imposing single-word-atomicity layout constraints (Java-style) that themselves restrict layout/SROA-class optimizer freedom. This uncarded interaction is decisive for Position 2's headline and for any P1 hybrid wanting bounded semantics in the unsafe fragment.

Design coupling vs reversibility: type-enforced sharing (P1) entangles concurrency with ownership/borrowing across the entire language and is near-irreversible if actor isolation or structured parallelism later proves superior; bounded semantics (P2) preserves design freedom in reserve at an ongoing, unmeasured residue tax.

Trusted-base placement, not elimination: every position bootstraps on permanently-trusted unsafe shared-memory code (P1's unsafe fragment and manual Send/Sync impls, P2's annotation/checker soundness, P3's systems module plus channel/scheduler runtime kernel). Its size, race semantics, and verification story are the same unresolved question in three costumes, and no advocate specified it.

Selection-biased corpus vs design-axis ranking: the non-interference-maximization framing all three positions share inherits the corpus's aliasing selection bias (caveats.md, misleading-comparisons entry 1); the evidence attests non-interference is valuable but cannot rank the sharing model against layout, numerics, dispatch, or IR design as the highest-leverage choice.
evidenceGaps: Empirical cost of bounded-race vs UB residue semantics on representative optimized kernels — explicitly 'Open' in implementation/concurrency-atomics-memory-model.md and in C002's negative scope; the decisive variable for the entire debate, unmeasured in either direction.

The analytical per-optimization delta accounting the critic proposed (which specific transforms differ between UB and bounded columns on ordinary non-atomic memory, derivable from C003/C005 primary sources before any benchmark) — attempted by nobody; would deflate or confirm the inflated cost models both P1 and P3 priced against.

Residue fraction: no evidence on what share of realistic optimizer-first workloads must live in unsafe/shared-mutation code, i.e., how much of the program the safe-fragment or core guarantee actually covers.

Specifiability of any bounded residue semantics: out-of-thin-air exclusion, mixed-size coherence, and torn-multiword memory safety have zero cards; this afflicts all three positions' escape hatches, not just Position 2.

Formal soundness of safe-fragment DRF and the interior-mutability/UnsafeCell aliasing model: C004 is provisional and Nomicon-sourced; RustBelt/Stacked-Borrows/Tree-Borrows-class artifacts are absent exactly where P1's soundness burden concentrates (caveats.md flags this fragment as unsettled).

Card series N001-N006, A001-A010, D001-D010, J001-J005 are cited as available in the ground rules but absent from all five provided files (verified: only F001-F009 and C001-C006 exist there); they must be located or formally struck before any case is treated as complete under the corpus's exact-card citation rule.

Actor/message-passing exemplars uncarded: Erlang, Pony reference capabilities, Swift actors, Ada tasking — explicitly Open in the implementation file; Position 3 argues entirely from adjacent evidence.

Structured parallelism as a fourth design point (fork-join, scoped regions, determinacy-race checking, disjoint-slice array mutation) — never argued by any advocate and uncarded, despite being arguably the dominant optimizer-first paradigm (SIMD/GPU offload).

Lowering fidelity: whether type/structural non-sharing facts survive the LLVM pipeline in practice; Rust's mutable-noalias enable/disable miscompile history is uncarded, and workflow-confirmed F009 (language-owned SIL-like IR as the semantics-preservation mitigation) was cited by nobody despite repricing every position's lowering risk.

Primary sources unfetched: C++ standard memory-model text and JLS chapter 17 (fetches blocked per implementation file); C001-C003 rest on a secondary survey (research.swtch.com) and the JSR-133 FAQ; all C-series cards remain provisional, not adversarially verified, despite being load-bearing for every position.
GPU/heterogeneous sync scopes, volatile/MMIO, signal-handler-visible memory, and MLIR async mapping — the implementation file's own mapping checklist — untouched by all three positions.

Threat model unstated: C002 records Java bounds races 'for security' (untrusted in-process code); whether this language will ever run untrusted code in-process largely determines the value of bounded outcomes, and no advocate engaged it.

Runtime cost of detect-and-halt race detection as a production mode, and the liveness failure class (deadlocks, unbounded queues, halts) that message passing and detect-and-halt trade races for — no cards, never weighed.
result.leadingCandidate: Position 1 (type-enforced data-race freedom via Send/Sync-like sharing capabilities plus exclusive borrowing) as the sharing-model core — with the residue question (UB vs bounded semantics for the unsafe/escape-hatch fragment, possibly a hybrid) explicitly unresolved and treated as a separable follow-on decision.
result.rationale: Three things are simultaneously true after the critic's pass. First, the positions substantively converge on the part the evidence supports: DRF-SC as the stated baseline (C001, uncontested) and mechanically checked non-sharing facts lowered to F003 noalias / C005 NotAtomic as the optimization fast path. That convergent kernel is backed by the corpus's only workflow-confirmed, adversarially-checked cards (F001, F002, F003) and is the most decision-ready element of the debate. Second, within that convergence, Position 1 leads: it is the mechanism the confirmed evidence most directly describes, it has a shipped existence proof (C004), and the critic's attacks on it were predominantly about rhetorical inflation ('theorem', 'only', broadened C003) rather than mechanism failure — whereas Position 2's distinctive premise (soundness without checker-strength types) was shown structurally incoherent under F003's UB-if-false contract plus the uncarded multiword-tearing problem, and Position 3's differentiation from Position 1 collapsed once the C006 immutable-sharing carve-out and residual-alias policing were shown to require borrow-checker-shaped machinery while leaving data-parallel workloads unhandled and its actual exemplars uncarded. Third — and decisive for the outcome label — the question the positions genuinely disagree on is the residue (what a race means where non-sharing cannot be proven), and there the corpus is empty on every load-bearing axis: no bounded-vs-UB cost data (C002 negative scope; 'Open' in the implementation file), no residue-fraction workload data, no specifiability evidence (out-of-thin-air, mixed-size, tearing), no formal safe-fragment soundness artifact behind C004, and no cards on the strongest un-argued alternative (structured parallelism) or on P3's exemplars. Additionally, the N/A/D/J card series the ground rules cite as available are absent from the provided files, so the debate ran on a verifiably partial deck, and all C-series cards are provisional. Under the corpus's own standing rules (no design decisions from this evidence base; every claim needs exact cards), 'design_candidate_later' would overstate: the leading candidate is leading on the convergent fast path, not on the contested residue, and an uncarded fourth design point could dominate. This is not evidence_conflict — the cards do not point opposite ways, they are silent at the decision point — and not prototype_needed alone, because several gaps (missing card series, primary sources, actor and structured-parallelism evidence, analytical delta accounting) precede and should shape any prototype. Outcome: research_needed, with Position 1 named as the working leading candidate that new evidence must either confirm or displace.
followUps: Locate or formally strike the missing card series (N001-N006, A001-A010, D001-D010, J001-J005); re-check all three cases against them before this topic is debated again.

Fetch and card the primary sources currently proxied by secondary ones: C++ standard [intro.races]/[atomics.order], JLS chapter 17, and Go memory model details; upgrade or correct C001-C003, and run adversarial verification on all C-series cards, which are load-bearing for every position.

Perform the analytical per-optimization UB-vs-bounded delta accounting from C003/C005 sources: enumerate exactly which transforms (read duplication/re-loading, cross-iteration caching of ordinary fields, speculation, invented writes) differ per column on ordinary non-atomic memory — closable now, without benchmarks.

Prototype/benchmark the residue tax: compile representative optimizer-first kernels with (a) full cross-thread non-sharing facts vs none, and (b) NotAtomic vs Unordered-class lowering of potentially-shared accesses, to bound the bounded-race cost empirically (the debate's declared decisive gap).

Card the uncarded competitor models: Pony reference capabilities, Erlang, Swift actors/strict-concurrency, Ada tasking; and add structured parallelism (Cilk determinacy races, DPJ regions, Rayon scoped threads/split borrows) as an explicit fourth position covering disjoint-slice data parallelism.

Card the formal-soundness and lowering-fidelity evidence P1 depends on: RustBelt / Stacked Borrows / Tree Borrows for the safe-fragment DRF and interior-mutability model, and Rust's production history of toggling mutable-noalias emission in LLVM.

Evaluate F009 (language-owned, semantics-preserving IR a la Swift SIL) as the mitigation for lowering-fidelity risk shared by all positions: decide whether sharing facts are consumed by a language-owned optimizer IR or encoded as fragile LLVM metadata.

Research residue specifiability directly: out-of-thin-air exclusion approaches (JMM revision history, promising semantics), mixed-size coherence, and the single-word-atomicity layout constraints required for memory safety under bounded races — including their own optimizer cost.

Gather workload evidence on the unsafe/shared-mutation fraction of realistic optimizer-first codebases (e.g., audit unsafe density in performance-critical Rust crates) to size the residue every position's guarantee excludes.

Decide the threat-model prior explicitly: will the language ever run untrusted code in-process (Java's stated motive for bounded races per C002)? This input reprices Position 2 substantially and should be settled before the residue-semantics decision.

Specify the trusted runtime kernel common to all positions (channels, scheduler, locks, atomics): its size, its internal race semantics, and its verification story — currently the same unresolved question in three costumes.

Work the implementation file's own mapping checklist: sync scopes (GPU/heterogeneous), volatile/MMIO, signal-handler-visible memory, and MLIR async representations, which sit outside all three DRF stories.
